title: Fix Renode Zephyr boot
description: The heterogeneous fleet runner now starts Renode 1.16.1 successfully on the validated ARM64 EC2 host and loads the nRF52840/SX1262 platform plus the T-Echo Zephyr ELF, but Zephyr produces no UART log file or lichen.telemetry.v1 events. Evidence from /tmp/hetero-fleet-1784134557: Renode reaches System bus created, remains alive, and exits only during cleanup; Python and Rust produce telemetry while Zephyr remains TX=0/RX=0. The ELF currently has _vector_start/_vector_table at 0x32200, initial SP 0x2000d8c0, reset PC 0x35d81; the runner has been updated to use VectorTableOffset 0x32200 and explicit SP/PC, without resolving silence. Investigate and fix the actual boot or console attachment failure, using instruction tracing or the previously validated T-Echo Renode harness as needed. Acceptance: a 20s one-Python/one-Rust/one-Zephyr run boots Zephyr, collects UART output, records nonzero Zephyr TX and RX, emits parseable lichen.telemetry.v1 events, and the analyzer identifies the Zephyr node and cross-implementation packet hashes. Also make the runner fail clearly when Renode or firmware boot fails.
design: 1. Reproduce with the exact previously validated Renode 1.16.1 ARM64 harness and capture Renode stdout, monitor state, UART backend existence, and CPU PC/SP. 2. Validate the uploaded ELF independently: program headers, _vector_start, initial SP, reset PC, flash offset, and that the remote ELF hash matches the local build. 3. Run a minimal Renode boot script with no SX1262 or simulator dependency, explicit PC/SP, CPU performance, and a known UART; prove a Zephyr boot banner before adding radio wiring. 4. Compare the minimal script against the current script one command at a time, especially platform include, LoadELF, vector setup, start order, UART backend, and DEVICEID tags. 5. Add a temporary machine-readable pre-radio boot marker only if Renode proves the CPU executes but logs are not routed; remove diagnostic-only code after root cause. 6. Reintroduce SX1262 and lichen-sim, then verify Zephyr TX/RX and lichen.telemetry.v1 events. Acceptance requires a 20s three-way run with Zephyr UART output, nonzero Zephyr TX/RX, packet hashes, and analyzer identification. Rejected approach: changing radio or telemetry logic before proving CPU execution, because current evidence shows no application output at all.
notes: Found and fixed stale reset PC: current ELF entry/reset vector is 0x35f1d while runner default was 0x35d81. Updated scripts/ec2-hetero-fleet.sh. Local Renode 1.16.1-equivalent one-node probe now emits *** Booting Zephyr OS build v3.7.0 *** to UART. EC2 three-way acceptance run still pending.
